mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding labor income and household spending power, which flows into corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of PAYEMS trend with 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate below 1.0%, persistent monetary accommodation compresses the equity discount rate and supports risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, the mean 20-day SPX forward return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) or the win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously low unemployment and rising consumer prices tighten the Fed reaction function, raising the expected path of policy rates and compressing equity multiples, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal from UNRATE and CPIAUCSL does not produce negative mean 60-day SPX returns (t-test p > 0.05) or the Rank IC exceeds -0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, accelerating industrial production signals resilient supply-side capacity that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS rising over prior 3 months), INDPRO growth fails to achieve a positive Rank IC greater than 0.03 with 20-day SPX returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production triggers negative earnings revisions and growth scare sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances of month-over-month INDPRO decline do not produce negative mean 5-day SPX returns (t-test p > 0.05) or the win rate exceeds 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts drive construction employment and durable goods demand, creating a positive wealth effect that lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of HOUST with 60-day SPX returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales exceed their 12-month moving average, accelerating consumer demand boosts revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS exceeds its 12-month moving average, the mean 20-day SPX forward return is not positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the hit ratio is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth surprises translate directly into aggregate demand expansion, lifting broad corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth does not exhibit positive Rank IC with 60-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.03).
